name: Swatantrata Sainik Samman Pension Scheme
benefit_description: Category of Freedom Fighters: Ex-Andaman Political/ Prisoners/ Spouses Basic Pension w.e.f. 15.08.2016 in Rupees (Per Month): 30,000/- Total Amount of Pension Including 3% DR in Rupees (Per Month): 30,900/- ﻿ Category of Freedom Fighters: Freedom Fighters Who Suffered Outside British India/Spouses B
details: Launched on 15th August 1972, "Swatantrata Sainik Samman Pension Scheme" is a Pension Scheme by the Ministry of Home Affairs. The scheme provides the grant of pension to living freedom fighters and their families; if they are no more alive, and to the families or martyrs. The benefits of the pension scheme are extended to all freedom fighters as a token of SAMMAN to them. ﻿ Duration Except in the case of unmarried daughters, the pension is for the lifetime of the recipient. In the case of unmarr
eligibility_text: For the purpose of granting a pension under the scheme, a freedom fighter is: (a) A person who had suffered a minimum imprisonment of six months in the mainland jails before Independence. However, ex-INA personnel will be eligible for pension if the imprisonment/detention suffered by them was outside India. The minimum period of actual imprisonment for pension eligibility has been reduced to three
application_url: Step 1: The interested applicant should request a free copy of the prescribed application form from either of two officers, i.e., the State Government/UT concerned or Freedom Fighters’ Division, Minis
